original_reasoning: Arabic تاه/هلك can conceptually drift into being lost, hidden, or gone from awareness, which aligns plausibly with λήθη-type concealment/forgetting.
Arabic gives disappearance or setting; a frontier or terminal place-name preserves the idea of an end point fairly well.
Arabic gives cutting off or stopping of breath, and Greek gives making inactive or abolishing; stoppage is a plausible shared core.
Corruption after cleanliness preserves a breakdown or dissolution idea with a plausible abstract drift.
Arabic sitting implies settling into a posture; Greek inclining/leaning shares the bodily posture core.
Arabic entices or draws someone out; Greek receives or takes in, a plausible take-receive chain.

Arabic is a specific date variety; fruit terminology often carries a ripe or fully-developed sense. Greek τελέεσθαι means to be completed or fulfilled, giving a plausible maturation-completion bridge.
Arabic qa'tara = 'fell, collapsed, came down'; Greek = 'come down, descend'. Plausible direct semantic echo of downward motion.
